title: Albert Park Circuit
sub: F1’s traditional season-opener: a fast, flowing semi-street circuit around a Melbourne lake. Reprofiled in 2022 into a quicker, wider layout with a rare four DRS zones.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Turn 1-3 braking complex off the main straight, then quick Turn 4-5 sweeps. DRS-heavy and racy.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: Reprofiled high-speed section, old chicane gone, a long flat-out run by the lake.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: Heavy braking at Turn 11, quick Turn 13 chicane, fast final corners onto the straight.

tyreAlloc: Softest in the range · C3 / C4 / C5
stratNote: Fast sweeps don’t punish tyres heavily; graining on a green opening-round surface is the bigger worry. Mostly a one-stopper unless the SC intervenes.
spots: End of the main straight + DRS. The classic lap-one and late-brake spot.
Heavy braking after a DRS zone; a strong secondary move.
End of the long flat-out run + DRS into a slow corner.
otNote: Historically dull, but the 2022 reprofile plus a rare four DRS zones (trimmed to three on safety grounds) turned it into a genuinely racy opener.
corners: Hard braking off the main straight + DRS. Prime overtaking and lap-one flashpoint.
Quick left flowing out of Turn 1.
Heavy braking after a DRS zone; strong secondary spot.
Fast right opening the flowing section.
High-speed right onto the reprofiled run.
Part of the flat-out lakeside sweeps.
Flowing left-right at high speed; reprofiled heart of the lap.
Heavy braking after a long flat-out run + DRS. Key zone.
Medium left through the stadium section.
Fast right-left flick; clip the kerbs.
Final right launching onto the main straight and DRS 1.
geo: A semi-permanent circuit on public roads around Albert Park Lake, just south of Melbourne’s CBD. Smooth, fast surface and a scenic backdrop.
Almost every corner widened or reshaped for 2022, the old Turn 9-10 chicane removed. Lap times dropped ~5s and racing improved.
Albert Park became the first circuit to run four DRS zones in 2022 (trimmed to three mid-weekend for safety).
Traditionally first on the calendar, so the grid’s first real read on the pecking order on a green track.
